text: Artificial intelligence is transforming our world, but it also raises important ethical questions. One major concern is bias, where AI systems can reflect unfair human prejudices, leading to decisions that discriminate against certain groups. To ensure fairness, developers must carefully test their algorithms. Privacy is another key issue, as AI often collects and analyses huge amounts of personal data. Users have the right to know how their information is used. This links to transparency—people should understand how AI makes decisions that affect their lives. Ultimately, responsible AI development requires balancing innovation with these ethical principles. By addressing bias, protecting privacy, and promoting transparency, we can build trust and create AI that benefits everyone responsibly.
